title: Session Termination on Logoff and VPN Disconnect

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 AC-12 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AC-12 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.23: Session Termination on Logoff and VPN Disconnect.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.23' running on cisco:asa VPN teardown, WinEventLog:Security 4634, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
spl:
index=net OR index=sec earliest=-24h (sourcetype=cisco:asa OR sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security)
| eval et=case(EventCode=4634,"win_logoff", match(_raw,"Teardown"),"vpn_teardown",1=1,null())
| stats count by et, user, src_ip
| sort - count
description: Correlates VPN connect/disconnect with OS logoff events to evidence AC-12 session termination when sessions end.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes net, sec and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, cisco:asa. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize VPN user field to match AD `user`; (2) alert on long VPN sessions without logoff (heuristic); (3) document remote access policy mapping to AC-17; (4) tune for split tunnel false positives. Indexes required: `index=net`, `index=sec`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=cisco:asa`.
visualization: Timeline (VPN vs logoff), Table (long sessions), Single value (disconnect anomalies). Key fields: `et`, `src_ip`, `user`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Access events during onboarding, M&A integration, controlled IT access reviews, or planned role transitions.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:asa` VPN teardown, `WinEventLog:Security` 4634.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize VPN user field to match AD `user`; (2) alert on long VPN sessions without logoff (heuristic); (3) document remote access policy mapping to AC-17; (4) tune for split tunnel false positives.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=net OR index=sec earliest=-24h (sourcetype=cisco:asa OR sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security)
| eval et=case(EventCode=4634,"win_logoff", match(_raw,"Teardown"),"vpn_teardown",1=1,null())
| stats count by et, user, src_ip
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Session Termination on Logoff and VPN Disconnect (AC-12)** — Correlates VPN connect/disconnect with OS logoff events to evidence AC-12 session termination when sessions end.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:asa` VPN teardown, `WinEventLog:Security` 4634. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: net, sec; **sourcetype**: cisco:asa, WinEventLog:Security. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=net, index=sec, sourcetype=cisco:asa, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **et** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by et, user, src_ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Session Termination on Logoff and VPN Disconnect (AC-12)** — Correlates VPN connect/disconnect with OS logoff events to evidence AC-12 session termination when sessions end.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:asa` VPN teardown, `WinEventLog:Security` 4634. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (VPN vs logoff), Table (long sessions), Single value (disconnect anomalies)
grandmaExplanation: We connect VPN connect/disconnect with OS logoff events to evidence AC-12 session termination when sessions end. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Authentication data model (Authentication.Authentication).
